parsed_papers.title: Pseudo-D: Informing Multi-View Uncertainty Estimation with Calibrated Neural Training Dynamics
parsed_papers.core_claim: Leveraging neural network training dynamics to generate calibrated uncertainty-aware pseudo-labels improves multi-view uncertainty estimation and robustness in medical image classification.
parsed_papers.problem_addressed: Current computer-aided diagnosis systems use oversimplified one-hot labels that ignore diagnostic uncertainty, forcing models to make overconfident predictions especially with noisy or ambiguous medical images.
key_contributions: Introducing a framework that uses neural network training dynamics (NNTD) to assess sample difficulty and generate uncertainty-aware pseudo-labels
Developing calibrated pseudo-label methods (Pseudo-T and Pseudo-D) that improve model calibration through temperature scaling and Dirichlet calibration
Demonstrating superior performance on echocardiography classification tasks with improved calibration, selective classification, and multi-view fusion
parsed_papers.novelty: Unlike prior works that use simple temperature scaling or ensemble approaches, this method specifically leverages the evolution of model predictions across training epochs to quantify sample difficulty and generate uncertainty-aware pseudo-labels, with Pseudo-D introducing class-wise scaling rather than global temperature scaling.
parsed_papers.method_summary: The method records model predictions across training epochs for each sample, aggregates these predictions into pseudo-labels representing model uncertainty, calibrates these pseudo-labels using validation data through temperature scaling or Dirichlet calibration, and trains models on these uncertainty-aware pseudo-labels to enhance robustness.
method_components: Neural network training dynamics (NNTD) aggregation using averaged logits across epochs
Temperature scaling (Pseudo-T) for basic calibration using validation cross-entropy minimization
Dirichlet calibration (Pseudo-D) with class-wise scaling matrix and bias using regularization
parsed_papers.key_innovation: Using the evolution of model predictions during training (rather than just final predictions) to quantify sample difficulty and generate uncertainty-aware pseudo-labels that reflect the ambiguity encountered during learning.
assumptions: The model's predictions during training reflect the inherent difficulty of each sample
Calibration on validation set generalizes to training set
Averaging logits across epochs provides a stable representation of model uncertainty
method_or_focus: Using the evolution of model predictions during training (rather than just final predictions) to quantify sample difficulty and generate uncertainty-aware pseudo-labels that reflect the ambiguity encountered during learning.
parsed_papers.hyperparameters: R(2+1)D model with Kinetics400 initialization, ADAM optimizer, learning rate 1e-4 for 20 epochs, random rotation ±15°, cropping ratio 0.7, temperature parameters γ, A, b tuned via SGD (lr 0.01, λ1=λ2=1)
parsed_papers.headline_result: Pseudo-D achieves best study-level AURC of 0.885 and MAE of 0.180
parsed_papers.best_result: Pseudo-D achieves best study-level AURC of 0.885 and MAE of 0.180
ablations: Pseudo-T vs Pseudo-D comparison shows Pseudo-D's class-wise calibration improves performance
parsed_papers.improvement_over_baseline: Pseudo-D improves study-level MAE by 19.6% (0.224→0.180) and AURC by 3.3% (0.852→0.885) over Vanilla baseline
unstated_weaknesses: Lack of specificity on how neural network training dynamics are quantified and aggregated
No mention of computational efficiency or scalability
No discussion of performance across different medical imaging modalities
threats_to_validity: Potential overfitting to specific types of medical images or conditions
Uncertainty estimates might not generalize across different medical domains
Method's performance under varying levels of label noise isn't specified
open_questions: How does the method perform with different neural network architectures?
What is the computational overhead compared to standard pseudo-labeling methods?
How does the method scale with larger datasets?
future_work: Extending to other medical imaging modalities
Integrating with active learning frameworks
gaps_this_enables: More nuanced pseudo-labeling in semi-supervised medical image classification
Better handling of diagnostic uncertainty in medical AI systems
Potential for more robust models under label noise conditions

parsed_papers.possible_gap: More nuanced pseudo-labeling in semi-supervised medical image classification

parsed_papers.title: Uncertainty Reactivation: Dynamic Contrastive Correction for Semi-Supervised Medical Image Segmentation
parsed_papers.core_claim: We propose the Dynamic Contrastive Correction Network (DCCN) to correct uncertain regions in pseudo-labels for semi-supervised medical image segmentation, rather than discarding them.
limitations: Existing methods discard or downweight uncertain regions, which often contain diagnostically important information like lesion cores or tissue boundaries.
parsed_papers.possible_gap: The gap is the need for methods that can effectively correct uncertain regions in pseudo-labels for semi-supervised medical segmentation, rather than simply discarding them.

parsed_papers.title: Sam-Driven Weakly Supervised Nodule Segmentation with Uncertainty-Aware Cross Teaching
parsed_papers.core_claim: A weakly supervised framework leveraging SAM's pseudo-label generation with uncertainty-aware cross-teaching can achieve accurate nodule segmentation using only aspect ratio annotations.
parsed_papers.problem_addressed: Existing nodule segmentation methods require costly and labor-intensive pixel-level annotations, limiting their practical application in ultrasound imaging.
key_contributions: Three types of bounding box prompts generated from clinical shape priors to enhance SAM's segmentation capabilities
SAM-driven uncertainty estimation method using prediction inconsistencies to identify unreliable regions
Uncertainty-aware cross-teaching strategy integrating label-space perturbations to mitigate pseudo-label noise
parsed_papers.novelty: Integration of SAM's uncertainty estimation with cross-teaching mechanism using pseudo-labels from shape-prior bounding boxes, specifically designed for ultrasound nodule segmentation under weak supervision.
parsed_papers.method_summary: The framework first generates three bounding box prompts (tight box, ellipse-based, and circle-based) from clinical annotations to guide SAM in producing pseudo-labels. It then selects optimal pseudo-labels using intersection/union operations and employs uncertainty-aware cross-teaching between two models trained on these labels.
method_components: Box Prompts Generation (BPG) module creating shape-prior bounding boxes
Pseudo-label selection module using intersection/union operations
SAM-driven uncertainty estimation computing XOR between intersection and union masks
Uncertainty-aware cross-teaching with dual models and label-space perturbations
parsed_papers.key_innovation: Using SAM's prediction inconsistencies (via XOR operation between intersection and union of pseudo-labels) to generate uncertainty maps that guide cross-teaching, effectively reducing the impact of pseudo-label inaccuracies during training.
assumptions: Nodules exhibit convex shapes that can be approximated by ellipses and circles
SAM's predictions, despite prompt sensitivity, contain sufficient signal for uncertainty estimation
Cross-teaching between two models can effectively leverage complementary pseudo-label information
method_or_focus: Using SAM's prediction inconsistencies (via XOR operation between intersection and union of pseudo-labels) to generate uncertainty maps that guide cross-teaching, effectively reducing the impact of pseudo-label inaccuracies during training.
Thyroid Ultrasound Dataset (422 left + 422 right images)

parsed_papers.title: Addressing Data Scarcity in 3D Trauma Detection through Self-Supervised and Semi-Supervised Learning with Vertex Relative Position Encoding
parsed_papers.core_claim: Self-supervised pre-training combined with semi-supervised learning can effectively address label scarcity in medical imaging, enabling robust 3D object detection with limited annotations.
parsed_papers.problem_addressed: The severe scarcity of annotated medical data for 3D trauma detection in abdominal CT scans.
key_contributions: Patch-based Masked Image Modeling (MIM) for self-supervised pre-training of a 3D U-Net encoder on 1,206 unlabeled CT volumes.
3D Vertex Relative Position Encoding (3D RPE) for 3D injury detection that overcomes limitations of center-based distance metrics.
parsed_papers.novelty: The paper introduces a novel combination of self-supervised pre-training with semi-supervised learning specifically for 3D medical image analysis, along with a new 3D RPE mechanism that computes geometric relationships between voxels and bounding box corners.
parsed_papers.method_summary: The approach uses patch-based Masked Image Modeling to pre-train a 3D U-Net encoder on unlabeled CT volumes, then employs this encoder for downstream tasks including 3D injury detection with VDETR and 3D RPE, and multi-label injury classification.
method_components: Patch-based Masked Image Modeling (MIM) for self-supervised pre-training.
3D U-Net encoder architecture.
VDETR with 3D Vertex Relative Position Encoding for detection.
Semi-supervised learning with consistency regularization.
parsed_papers.key_innovation: The 3D Vertex Relative Position Encoding (3D RPE) mechanism, which computes geometric relationships between each voxel and all eight corners of the predicted bounding box, addressing the insufficiency of center-based distance metrics in 3D medical imaging.
assumptions: The pre-trained encoder features are transferable to downstream tasks.
Masked reconstruction provides sufficient supervision for learning anatomical representations.
Semi-supervised learning with consistency regularization can leverage unlabeled data effectively.
method_or_focus: The 3D Vertex Relative Position Encoding (3D RPE) mechanism, which computes geometric relationships between each voxel and all eight corners of the predicted bounding box, addressing the insufficiency of center-based distance metrics in 3D medical imaging.

parsed_papers.title: Mutual Evidential Deep Learning for Medical Image Segmentation
parsed_papers.core_claim: The proposed Mutual Evidential Deep Learning (MEDL) framework achieves state-of-the-art performance by generating reliable pseudo-labels through complementary evidence from dual architectures and uncertainty-guided learning.
parsed_papers.problem_addressed: Existing semi-supervised medical segmentation frameworks suffer from performance degradation due to biases from low-quality pseudo-labels, as their averaging-based integration fails to account for pseudo-label reliability from different sources.
key_contributions: Dual-network architecture generating complementary evidence for unlabeled samples with class-aware evidential fusion
Asymptotic Fisher information-based evidential learning strategy for uncertainty-driven sample weighting
Reliability-based pseudo-label masking using Shannon entropy to reduce misleading signals
parsed_papers.novelty: Unlike prior methods that average pseudo-labels, MEDL dynamically evaluates and weights pseudo-label reliability using uncertainty measures from dual networks, enabling curriculum-style learning that focuses on reliable samples first.
parsed_papers.method_summary: MEDL employs two segmentation networks with different architectures to generate complementary predictions for unlabeled data, fuses them via class-aware evidential fusion, calculates reliability using Shannon entropy, and applies Fisher information-based uncertainty weighting to guide targeted learning.

parsed_papers.title: Semi-supervised learning for medical image classification using imbalanced training data
parsed_papers.core_claim: The Adaptive Blended Consistency Loss (ABCL) effectively counteracts data skew in imbalanced medical image classification by adaptively blending target class distributions based on class frequencies.
parsed_papers.problem_addressed: Semi-supervised learning for medical image classification faces challenges with class imbalance due to scarcity of disease-positive individuals, which existing SSL methods don't adequately address.
key_contributions: Proposed Adaptive Blended Consistency Loss (ABCL) as a drop-in replacement for consistency loss in perturbation-based SSL methods
Demonstrated improvements to unweighted average recall on two imbalanced medical image classification datasets
parsed_papers.novelty: Unlike existing consistency losses (CL and SCL) that always target the original sample prediction (OSP) and don't account for class imbalance, ABCL adaptively blends OSP and augmented sample predictions (ASP) based on class frequencies to counteract bias toward majority classes.
parsed_papers.method_summary: ABCL creates a blended target distribution that combines OSP and ASP predictions with weighting determined by class frequencies, pulling both original and augmented predictions toward this target distribution using Kullback-Leibler divergence loss.
method_components: ABCL loss function: L_ABCL(z, ŷ) = L_con(z_blended, z) + L_con(z_blended, ŷ)
Blended target distribution: z_blended = (1-k) * z + k * ŷ
Weighting calculation: k = max(0, min(γ * (N_original - N_augmented) + 0.5, 1))
parsed_papers.key_innovation: Adaptive weighting of target distributions based on class frequencies to counteract natural bias toward majority classes, with k < 0.5 when OSP is minority class and k > 0.5 when ASP is minority class.
assumptions: The model's predictions for original and augmented samples can be meaningfully combined
Class frequency information can effectively guide the blending of target distributions

parsed_papers.problem_addressed: Semi-supervised medical image segmentation suffers from limited labeled data, where existing methods rely on direct pseudo supervision of predictions rather than feature-level learning.
key_contributions: Stage-adaptive contrastive learning framework with boundary-aware loss (first stage) and prototype-aware loss (second stage)
Aleatoric Uncertainty Adaptive (AUA) method for generating higher-quality pseudo labels by adaptively regularizing prediction consistency

AUA estimates aleatoric uncertainty to adaptively weight consistency regularization between student and teacher models.
method_components: Aleatoric Uncertainty Adaptive (AUA) module with uncertainty estimation and adaptive consistency regularization

Low-rank approximation of covariance matrix is computationally feasible
Pseudo labels become more accurate in the second stage with better feature representations

parsed_papers.title: Domain-Specific, Semi-Supervised Transfer Learning for Medical Imaging
parsed_papers.core_claim: A lightweight architecture (MAKNet) trained with semi-supervised pseudo-labels achieves superior performance with 60-70% fewer parameters compared to popular architectures.
parsed_papers.problem_addressed: Limited availability of annotated medical imaging data and the ineffectiveness of transfer learning from natural image domains to medical tasks.

Semi-supervised learning pipeline using teacher-student framework to generate pseudo-labels from large unlabeled medical dataset
Demonstrated importance of domain-specific knowledge for effective transfer learning in medical imaging

parsed_papers.title: Uncertainty-Aware Extreme Point Tracing for Weakly Supervised Ultrasound Image Segmentation
parsed_papers.core_claim: A weakly supervised segmentation framework using only four extreme points as annotation can achieve performance comparable to or surpassing fully supervised methods while significantly reducing annotation cost.
parsed_papers.problem_addressed: Fully supervised medical image segmentation requires extensive pixel-level annotations that are costly and time-consuming, making large-scale annotation impractical for clinical workflows.
key_contributions: A weakly supervised segmentation framework leveraging only four extreme points as annotation
An enhanced Feature-Guided Extreme Point Masking (FGEPM) algorithm incorporating Monte Carlo dropout-based uncertainty estimation
A dual-branch Uncertainty-aware Scale Consistency (USC) loss and box alignment loss for spatial consistency
parsed_papers.novelty: Integration of Monte Carlo dropout-based uncertainty estimation into FGEPM to construct a unified gradient-uncertainty cost map, and the dual-branch USC loss that selectively applies stronger consistency supervision to confident regions while down-weighting ambiguous areas.
parsed_papers.method_summary: The framework uses SAM2 with bounding box prompts derived from four extreme points to generate initial pseudo labels, which are progressively refined using an enhanced FGEPM algorithm with Monte Carlo dropout-based uncertainty estimation, while enforcing spatial consistency with USC loss and boundary alignment with box alignment loss.
method_components: Initial pseudo-label generation using SAM2 with bounding box prompts
Uncertainty-aware Scale Consistency (USC) loss
Box alignment loss
Monte Carlo dropout-based uncertainty estimation
Feature-Guided Extreme Point Masking (FGEPM) algorithm
parsed_papers.key_innovation: The construction of a unified gradient-uncertainty cost map using Monte Carlo dropout-based uncertainty estimation within the FGEPM algorithm, which allows contours to be traced more precisely from extreme points and ensures pseudo labels become progressively more accurate during training.

SAM2 can effectively segment lesions when provided with bounding box prompts derived from extreme points
Monte Carlo dropout provides meaningful uncertainty estimates for pixel-wise refinement

parsed_papers.title: DRIVE: Dual-Robustness via Information Variability and Entropic Consistency in Source-Free Unsupervised Domain Adaptation
parsed_papers.core_claim: DRIVE introduces a dual-model framework with dual-robustness through information variability and entropic consistency to enhance generalization and robustness in SFUDA.
parsed_papers.problem_addressed: Source-Free Unsupervised Domain Adaptation (SFUDA) where models must adapt to new domains without labeled source data, leading to overfitting, underfitting, and poor generalization due to domain discrepancies and noise.
key_contributions: A dual-model architecture with parallel models initialized identically to capture diverse target domain characteristics
An entropy-aware pseudo-labeling strategy that adjusts label weights based on prediction uncertainty
parsed_papers.novelty: Unlike prior SFUDA methods that rely on single-model architectures, DRIVE introduces a dual-model structure with both consistency and divergence regularizations, leveraging mutual information to enhance robustness across diverse target conditions.
parsed_papers.method_summary: DRIVE employs a dual-model, two-stage approach with perturbation-induced robustness using Projected Gradient Descent (PGD) noise perturbations. Stage 1 aligns models on stable features using mutual information consistency loss, while Stage 2 dynamically adjusts perturbation levels based on Stage 1 losses.
method_components: Projected Gradient Descent (PGD) perturbations guided by mutual information
Entropy-aware pseudo-labeling strategy
Mutual information consistency loss for model alignment
parsed_papers.key_innovation: The dual-phase application of mutual information—first as a consistency mechanism and then as a divergence mechanism—enables robust feature alignment while promoting model flexibility across diverse target conditions.
assumptions: The source and target domains share common classes
The pre-trained source model can be adapted using only unlabeled target data
method_or_focus: The dual-phase application of mutual information—first as a consistency mechanism and then as a divergence mechanism—enables robust feature alignment while promoting model flexibility across diverse target conditions.

parsed_papers.title: Anomaly Detection in Medical Imaging - A Mini Review
parsed_papers.core_claim: Anomaly detection methods effectively address medical imaging tasks like classification and segmentation by leveraging semi-supervised and unsupervised learning, reducing dependence on costly labeled data.
parsed_papers.problem_addressed: The difficulty of obtaining reliable labeled data for supervised machine learning in medical imaging, necessitating semi-supervised or unsupervised approaches like anomaly detection.
key_contributions: Semi-exhaustive literature review of 120 papers on anomaly detection in medical imaging, clustered into applications (ocular, chest X-ray/mammography, CT/fMRI, brain MRI, mixed domains).
Identification of dominant research trends: brain MRI applications (53.85% of papers), prevalence of deviation-based methods using autoencoders/GANs (69.23%), and dataset-driven research bias.
Demonstration of successful applications in tumor detection (7/15 brain MRI papers), pneumonia/COVID-19 detection in chest X-rays, and retina lesion segmentation in OCT.
parsed_papers.novelty: Synthesis of lessons learned from 120 papers to establish domain-specific trends, methodological preferences (e.g., AEs/GANs), and practical advice for anomaly detection in medical imaging, highlighting the link between data availability and research focus.
parsed_papers.method_summary: Qualitative literature review using Google Scholar and 4 search terms, filtering 120 papers to 45 relevant research items, then clustering by imaging modality and analyzing applications, methods, and motivations.
method_components: Semi-exhaustive literature search and filtering
Categorization of papers by imaging modality (Tables I-V)
Analysis of applied methods (e.g., AEs, GANs, CNNs)
Identification of research motivations (e.g., labeled data scarcity)
parsed_papers.key_innovation: The systematic clustering of anomaly detection applications across medical imaging domains, revealing the dominance of brain MRI research and deviation-based methods, and linking research trends to dataset availability.
assumptions: Anomalies in medical images can be defined as deviations from normal/healthy samples.
Publicly available datasets drive research focus in specific imaging domains.
Semi-supervised/unsupervised methods can effectively replace supervised approaches where labels are scarce.
method_or_focus: The systematic clustering of anomaly detection applications across medical imaging domains, revealing the dominance of brain MRI research and deviation-based methods, and linking research trends to dataset availability.
datasets_used: CT scans of skeleton or spines

Cross-task consistency loss (segmentation-regression alignment)

parsed_papers.title: Less Is More: Surgical Phase Recognition From Timestamp Supervision
parsed_papers.core_claim: Timestamp supervision (single timestamp per phase) with uncertainty-aware temporal diffusion achieves competitive performance to full supervision while reducing annotation cost by 74%.
parsed_papers.problem_addressed: Surgical phase recognition requires expensive full annotations where surgeons mark precise start/end times for each phase, creating a barrier for practical deployment.
key_contributions: Introduces timestamp supervision paradigm for surgical phase recognition
Proposes Uncertainty-Aware Temporal Diffusion (UATD) method to generate pseudo labels from timestamps
Demonstrates loop training strategy to handle long videos and class imbalance
parsed_papers.novelty: UATD uniquely combines temporal diffusion guided by uncertainty estimation to expand single timestamps to high-confidence neighboring frames, avoiding boundary ambiguity while leveraging the consecutive nature of surgical phases.
parsed_papers.method_summary: Uses Monte Carlo Dropout for uncertainty estimation, diffuses timestamp labels to adjacent low-uncertainty frames, and employs loop training to optimize spatial and temporal extractors separately using only labeled frames.
method_components: Uncertainty estimation via Monte Carlo Dropout
Temporal diffusion algorithm with uncertainty thresholding
Loop training decoupling spatial/temporal optimization
parsed_papers.key_innovation: Temporal diffusion stops at high-uncertainty frames (boundaries), ensuring generated pseudo labels are high-confidence and phase-consistent, enabling effective learning from minimal supervision.
assumptions: Surgical phases are long events with consecutive frames
Uncertainty estimation via Monte Carlo Dropout reliably identifies high-confidence frames
Surgeons tend to annotate timestamps near phase midpoints
method_or_focus: Temporal diffusion stops at high-uncertainty frames (boundaries), ensuring generated pseudo labels are high-confidence and phase-consistent, enabling effective learning from minimal supervision.
limitations: The method is specifically designed for surgical videos where phases are long events, which might limit its applicability to other medical image classification tasks.
unstated_weaknesses: The method's performance under significant label noise is not addressed.
The method's reliance on temporal information might not be suitable for static medical images.
The paper doesn't discuss how the method handles variations in surgical procedures or surgeons.

parsed_papers.title: Complementing Semi-Supervised Learning with Uncertainty Quantification
parsed_papers.core_claim: Complementing SSL with an uncertainty-aware objective that quantifies both aleatoric and epistemic uncertainty improves performance by aligning uncertainty distributions between labeled and unlabeled domains.
parsed_papers.problem_addressed: Existing semi-supervised learning methods do not incorporate uncertainty quantification for pseudo-labels or unlabeled samples, leading to issues with noisy labels and out-of-distribution samples due to strong augmentations.
key_contributions: Proposing a novel uncertainty-aware objective incorporating both aleatoric and epistemic uncertainty for SSL
Implementing the approach by complementing contemporary SSL work with the uncertainty framework
Achieving state-of-the-art performance on standard SSL benchmarks including CIFAR-100 and Mini-ImageNet
parsed_papers.novelty: This is the first work to estimate and align uncertainty distributions of labeled and unlabeled data in SSL, whereas previous efforts focused solely on consistency regularization without uncertainty quantification.
parsed_papers.method_summary: The approach proposes an unsupervised uncertainty-aware objective that quantifies aleatoric and epistemic uncertainty to align distributions between labeled and unlabeled domains, complementing existing SSL techniques without requiring computationally expensive graph formation or similarity computations.
method_components: Pseudo-labeling mechanism with confidence thresholding
Consistency regularization between weakly and strongly augmented samples
Uncertainty quantification using both aleatoric and epistemic measures
parsed_papers.key_innovation: Incorporating uncertainty quantification into the SSL training process to handle noisy pseudo-labels and out-of-distribution samples by aligning uncertainty distributions between labeled and unlabeled domains.
assumptions: Unlabeled data has similar distribution to labeled data
Uncertainty quantification can effectively identify problematic samples
Strong augmentations may introduce out-of-distribution samples
method_or_focus: Incorporating uncertainty quantification into the SSL training process to handle noisy pseudo-labels and out-of-distribution samples by aligning uncertainty distributions between labeled and unlabeled domains.

parsed_papers.improvement_over_baseline: 0.08% over SimPLE on CIFAR-10 (1000 labels); 0.07% over SimPLE on CIFAR-100 (4000 labels)

parsed_papers.problem_addressed: Few-shot segmentation struggles with limited annotated samples, making it difficult to model appearance variations and diverse visual cues between query and support images.
key_contributions: Leveraging unlabeled images to generate additional prototypes
Using uncertainty estimates to refine pseudo labels and exclude unreliable predictions

Pseudo label refinement by excluding high-uncertainty predictions
Combining support and unlabeled prototypes for query segmentation

parsed_papers.core_claim: The Uncertainty-aware Distillation with Class-Equilibrium (UaD-CE) framework effectively resolves Semi-FSCIL challenges by combining class-balanced self-training and uncertainty-aware distillation.
parsed_papers.problem_addressed: The paper addresses the adaptability issue of semi-supervised learning techniques to Few-Shot Class-Incremental Learning (FSCIL) tasks, where unlabeled data is incorporated to compensate for limited labeled samples but causes instability in knowledge distillation.
key_contributions: Proposed UaD-CE framework integrating Uncertainty-aware Distillation (UaD) and Class-Equilibrium (CE) modules
Implemented class-balanced self-training to prevent easy-to-classify classes from dominating pseudo-label generation
Developed uncertainty-guided knowledge refinement with adaptive distillation for stable knowledge transfer
parsed_papers.novelty: Unlike prior Semi-FSCIL work that heuristically applied semi-supervised techniques, UaD-CE specifically addresses FSCIL challenges through (1) class-level pseudo-label selection based on prediction probabilities (Equation 3) and (2) uncertainty-aware distillation that handles model instability from unlabeled samples during distillation.
parsed_papers.method_summary: The framework operates in incremental sessions: (1) CE module uses class-balanced self-training to incorporate unlabeled data while preventing class imbalance; (2) UaD module performs uncertainty-aware distillation between reference and target models using exemplars; (3) exemplars are updated by filtering samples from current datasets. The loss function combines cross-entropy and distillation losses (Equation 4).
method_components: Class-Equilibrium (CE) module: Class-balanced self-training with pseudo-label selection based on class difficulty
Uncertainty-aware Distillation (UaD) module: Knowledge distillation with uncertainty handling
Exemplar management: Dynamic updating of old class samples for knowledge transfer
parsed_papers.key_innovation: The class-balanced self-training mechanism that selects unlabeled samples based on inverse class difficulty (Equation 3), ensuring hard-to-classify classes receive more pseudo-label samples to prevent dominance by easy classes during semi-supervised learning.
assumptions: Unlabeled data contains useful information for learning novel classes
Model uncertainty can be estimated from prediction probabilities
Class difficulty correlates with prediction confidence
Knowledge distillation can effectively transfer knowledge between models
method_or_focus: The class-balanced self-training mechanism that selects unlabeled samples based on inverse class difficulty (Equation 3), ensuring hard-to-classify classes receive more pseudo-label samples to prevent dominance by easy classes during semi-supervised learning.
parsed_papers.headline_result: UaD-CE achieves 54.50% on CIFAR100, 50.52% on miniImageNet, and 60.72% on CUB200 in the final session
parsed_papers.best_result: UaD-CE achieves 54.50% on CIFAR100, 50.52% on miniImageNet, and 60.72% on CUB200 in the final session
ablations: UaD module vs Standard Knowledge Distillation (S_DL): UaD improves accuracy across all incremental sessions
CE module vs Traditional Self-Training: CE reduces performance decline (slight decline vs significant drop)
Impact of unlabeled samples: UaD-CE shows greater boost from unlabeled data compared to Semi-CEC/Semi-SPPR
parsed_papers.improvement_over_baseline: UaD-CE outperforms state-of-the-art by ~4% on CIFAR100 and ~3% on CUB200 in final session; achieves 14.45% PD on CUB200 vs 23.56% for Semi-CEC
unstated_weaknesses: Focuses only on adapting semi-supervised learning to FSCIL without addressing other challenges in semi-supervised learning
Limited evaluation on diverse datasets and scenarios
Potential overfitting to specific datasets used in evaluation
threats_to_validity: Generalization to other domains and datasets is uncertain
Potential overfitting to the specific datasets used in the evaluation
open_questions: How does the method perform in other domains beyond vision tasks?
What are the computational requirements and scalability of the method?
future_work: Extending the method to other domains and applications
Investigating the computational efficiency and scalability of the method
gaps_this_enables: Research on adapting semi-supervised learning techniques to incremental learning scenarios
Exploration of uncertainty-aware techniques in few-shot learning
parsed_papers.relevance_to_topic: The paper's focus on uncertainty-aware distillation for semi-supervised learning provides insights that could be adapted for uncertainty-aware pseudo-label selection in medical image classification.
parsed_papers.possible_gap: Research on adapting semi-supervised learning techniques to incremental learning scenarios

parsed_papers.title: Uncertainty-aware Parameter-Efficient Self-training for Semi-supervised Language Understanding
parsed_papers.core_claim: This paper presents UPE, an uncertainty-aware and parameter-efficient self-training method to improve the performance of large pre-trained language models in low-resource scenarios.
limitations: Self-training is limited by noisy labels and high computational cost due to updating all model parameters.
parsed_papers.possible_gap: The paper does not detail how the uncertainty estimation is implemented or whether the parameter-efficient approach significantly limits the model's final performance compared to full fine-tuning.

parsed_papers.title: Self-supervised learning and semi-supervised learning for multi-sequence medical image classification
parsed_papers.core_claim: Self-supervised and semi-supervised learning are effective approaches for multi-sequence medical data analysis.
limitations: Performance may not match fully supervised models on large labeled datasets.
parsed_papers.possible_gap: The challenge of leveraging large amounts of unlabeled multi-sequence medical data for effective model training.

parsed_papers.title: Pixel Exclusion: Uncertainty-aware Boundary Discovery for Active Cross-Domain Semantic Segmentation
parsed_papers.core_claim: This paper proposes an Active Learning method to improve cross-domain semantic segmentation by selecting informative pixels for annotation, thereby mitigating the issue of noisy pseudo-labels and reducing the performance gap with fully-supervised models.
limitations: Generated pseudo-labels are noisy without external supervision.
Performance gap between UDA models and fully-supervised models.
parsed_papers.possible_gap: The performance gap between Unsupervised Domain Adaptation models and fully-supervised models remains a significant open challenge.

parsed_papers.title: Conformal Credal Self-Supervised Learning
parsed_papers.core_claim: The paper proposes a conformal credal self-supervised learning method that addresses the lack of validity in pseudo-labels by using sets of probability distributions instead of single ones.
limitations: Pseudo-labels from existing self-training methods are based on ad-hoc heuristics and lack validity guarantees.
parsed_papers.possible_gap: The abstract introduces the 'credal' aspect but does not detail the 'conformal' guarantees; a gap is to empirically validate the statistical validity provided by the conformal component in this new learning paradigm.

parsed_papers.title: Aggregated Mutual Learning between CNN and Transformer for semi-supervised medical image segmentation
parsed_papers.core_claim: Aggregated Mutual Learning between CNN and Transformer models can effectively improve performance in semi-supervised medical image analysis.
limitations: Performance is highly dependent on the quality and quantity of the unlabeled data.
parsed_papers.possible_gap: The paper does not provide a theoretical analysis of why the mutual learning between CNN and Transformer is effective.

parsed_papers.title: Exploring Text-Enhanced Mixture-of-Experts for Semi-supervised Medical Image Segmentation with Composite Data
parsed_papers.core_claim: Integrating natural language descriptions with a Mixture-of-Experts architecture significantly improves the performance of semi-supervised medical image segmentation.
limitations: Performance is highly dependent on the quality and specificity of the text prompts.
parsed_papers.possible_gap: The paper does not investigate how the model performs with noisy, ambiguous, or contradictory text prompts, which is a critical real-world scenario.

parsed_papers.title: Semantic knowledge transfer for semi-supervised medical image segmentation
parsed_papers.core_claim: This research introduces a method for semi-supervised medical image segmentation by transferring semantic knowledge.
limitations: The method's performance may be highly dependent on the quality of the initial labeled data.
parsed_papers.possible_gap: The paper does not explore how different types of semantic knowledge (e.g., anatomical vs. pathological) affect the transfer process.

parsed_papers.title: PTCL: Pseudo-Label Temporal Curriculum Learning for Label-Limited Dynamic Graph
parsed_papers.core_claim: This paper proposes PTCL, a pseudo-label temporal curriculum learning method to enable dynamic node classification in scenarios with limited and delayed labels.
limitations: High annotation costs and label uncertainty (e.g., ambiguous or delayed labels) in real-world dynamic systems.
parsed_papers.possible_gap: The method assumes final timestamp labels are available; a gap would be developing techniques for scenarios where even these final labels are missing or highly uncertain.

parsed_papers.title: Uncertainty-Aware Adaptive Pseudo-Labeling for Referring Video Object Segmentation
parsed_papers.core_claim: This paper introduces a novel pseudo-labeling method that is both uncertainty-aware and adaptive to improve referring video object segmentation.
limitations: May struggle with highly occluded or fast-moving objects over long video sequences.
parsed_papers.possible_gap: The method's performance on out-of-distribution videos or with highly complex and ambiguous referring expressions is not evaluated.

parsed_papers.title: Semi-Supervised Adversarial Self-Integration Network for Medical Image Segmentation
parsed_papers.core_claim: A novel semi-supervised adversarial self-integration network is proposed to overcome the limitations of traditional U-Net architectures.
method_or_focus: Semi-supervised learning by integrating CNNs and Vision Transformers
Adversarial consistency training with two consistency-based discriminators
limitations: Ineffective context extraction
Restricted receptive fields
parsed_papers.possible_gap: The abstract does not provide any quantitative results or performance comparisons to validate the proposed method's effectiveness.

parsed_papers.title: CTS: Sim-to-Real Unsupervised Domain Adaptation on 3D Detection
parsed_papers.core_claim: This paper presents a novel Complex-to-Simple (CTS) framework to transfer models.
limitations: The dramatic performance drop caused by sim-to-real domain inconsistencies.
parsed_papers.possible_gap: Progress in sim-to-real unsupervised domain adaptation remains limited compared to real-to-real tasks.

parsed_papers.title: Semi-supervised Medical Image Segmentation with Strong/Weak Task-Aware Consistency
parsed_papers.core_claim: Semi-supervised Medical Image Segmentation with Strong/Weak Task-Aware Consistency
limitations: Dependence on the quality and quantity of unlabeled data
parsed_papers.possible_gap: Existing semi-supervised methods for medical image segmentation often apply generic consistency constraints that are not tailored to the specific segmentation task.

parsed_papers.title: Exploring Smoothness and Class-Separation for Semi-supervised Medical Image Segmentation
parsed_papers.core_claim: The paper proposes the SS-Net to address challenges in semi-supervised medical image segmentation by applying smoothness constraints and encouraging class-level separation.
limitations: Scarcity of annotated data and blurred pixels in low-contrast regions.
parsed_papers.possible_gap: The paper focuses on smoothness but may not fully address the challenge of preserving fine, detailed boundaries in segmentation.

parsed_papers.title: Uncertainty-aware pseudo-labeling for quantum calculations
parsed_papers.core_claim: This paper introduces an uncertainty-aware pseudo-labeling method to accelerate quantum calculations while maintaining accuracy.
method_or_focus: Machine Learning model for quantum property prediction
limitations: The method's performance is highly dependent on the quality of the initial model and its pseudo-labels, which can introduce and amplify errors.
parsed_papers.possible_gap: The research does not explore the method's applicability to more complex quantum systems, such as transition states or periodic materials, which may exhibit different uncertainty profiles.

parsed_papers.title: Momentum Contrastive Voxel-wise Representation Distillation for Semi-supervised Volumetric Medical Image Segmentation
parsed_papers.core_claim: This paper proposes a Momentum Contrastive Voxel-wise Representation Distillation method for effective semi-supervised learning.
limitations: Not specified in the title
parsed_papers.possible_gap: The title is generic and doesn't specify the target application or task for the semi-supervised learning.

parsed_papers.title: HiProto: Hierarchical Prototype Learning for Interpretable Object Detection Under Low-quality Conditions
parsed_papers.core_claim: We propose HiProto, a method that uses hierarchical prototype learning to achieve interpretable object detection, especially under low-quality imaging conditions.
limitations: Existing methods lack interpretability
Existing methods fail to improve semantic discrimination
parsed_papers.possible_gap: It is unclear if combining HiProto's prototype learning with existing image enhancement techniques could further improve performance under low-quality conditions.

parsed_papers.title: HSIC-based Moving Weight Averaging for Few-Shot Open-Set Object Detection
parsed_papers.core_claim: This paper proposes a new method for few-shot open-set object detection that uses evidential uncertainty from a Dirichlet distribution to improve performance in data-scarce scenarios.
method_or_focus: Evidential Uncertainty Estimation via Dirichlet Distribution
limitations: Lack of tailored considerations for data-scarce scenarios in previous works
parsed_papers.possible_gap: The abstract is incomplete, making it difficult to fully assess the proposed methods, datasets, and results.

parsed_papers.title: Decoupled Semantic Prototypes enable learning from diverse annotation types for semi-weakly segmentation in expert-driven domains
parsed_papers.core_claim: Decoupled Semantic Prototypes enable learning from diverse annotation types, making segmentation more affordable for expert-driven domains like medical imaging.
method_or_focus: Training strategies for diverse annotation types
limitations: Limited availability of domain experts for pixel-wise annotations
Difficulty of transferring segmentation models to expert-driven domains
parsed_papers.possible_gap: The paper focuses on affordability but may not fully address the potential trade-off in model performance or accuracy when using cheaper, diverse annotations over high-quality pixel-wise ones.

parsed_papers.title: Leveraging Self-Supervision for Cross-Domain Crowd Counting
parsed_papers.core_claim: The paper claims that combining synthetic data with self-supervision can effectively address the domain shift problem in cross-domain crowd counting.
limitations: Heavy reliance on manually annotated data
Poor generalization due to domain shift when using synthetic data
parsed_papers.possible_gap: The abstract is incomplete, leaving the specific details of how self-supervision is integrated with synthetic data unclear.

parsed_papers.title: MedGS: Gaussian Splatting for Multi-Modal 3D Medical Imaging
parsed_papers.core_claim: MedGS is a novel method that uses Gaussian Splatting to effectively represent and visualize multi-modal 3D medical images.
limitations: The method may face challenges in handling the noise and varying resolutions typical of real-world medical scans.
parsed_papers.possible_gap: There is a need for more efficient and high-fidelity methods for real-time visualization and fusion of multi-modal 3D medical data.

parsed_papers.title: Supplementary: Uncertainty-Aware Adaptation for Self-Supervised 3D Human Pose Estimation
parsed_papers.core_claim: This paper presents a method for uncertainty-aware adaptation in self-supervised 3D human pose estimation.
parsed_papers.possible_gap: Need to download and read the full PDF to verify claims.

parsed_papers.title: Rethinking Weak-to-Strong Augmentation in Source-Free Domain Adaptive Object Detection
parsed_papers.core_claim: The paper argues that the standard weak-to-strong augmentation strategy is ineffective for source-free domain adaptive object detection.
limitations: The source-free constraint makes the adaptation task significantly more challenging.
parsed_papers.possible_gap: The paper identifies a gap in applying effective data augmentation techniques, specifically weak-to-strong, to the challenging source-free domain adaptation setting.

research_gaps.gap_title: Uncertainty-calibrated pseudo-label acceptance under label noise
research_gaps.text: The evidence repeatedly studies uncertainty-aware semi-supervised medical imaging, but the selection rule for accepting pseudo-labels under noisy labeled seeds is still under-tested. A local proxy can test whether entropy, margin, and agreement filters reduce noisy pseudo-label promotion compared with naive confidence thresholding.
research_gaps.testable_hypothesis: Entropy-filtered, class-balanced pseudo-label selection will improve held-out classification accuracy over naive confidence pseudo-labeling when the initial labeled set contains injected label noise.
research_gaps.suggested_dataset: sklearn_digits medical-image SSL proxy with controlled label noise; remote follow-up: MedMNIST/BreastMNIST or organ-specific medical image datasets.
research_gaps.baseline_paper: [EV001] Pseudo-D: Informing Multi-View Uncertainty Estimation with Calibrated Neural Training Dynamics

research_gaps.gap_title: Class-balanced pseudo-labeling for scarce medical labels
research_gaps.text: Several papers report gains from SSL, but small medical datasets can amplify class imbalance and confirmation bias. The gap is whether pseudo-label selection should explicitly balance classes instead of only using max probability.
research_gaps.testable_hypothesis: Class-balanced low-entropy selection will reduce minority-class collapse and improve macro/weighted F1 against max-confidence selection.
research_gaps.suggested_dataset: sklearn_digits SSL proxy with small labeled-per-class budget; remote follow-up: imbalanced medical image classification dataset.
research_gaps.baseline_paper: [EV001] Pseudo-D: Informing Multi-View Uncertainty Estimation with Calibrated Neural Training Dynamics

research_gaps.gap_title: Calibration versus accuracy tradeoff in medical pseudo-label selection
research_gaps.text: Uncertainty-aware methods often optimize accuracy-like metrics, while medical deployment needs calibrated confidence. The gap is to measure whether pseudo-label filters improve accuracy while preserving calibration under noisy labels.
research_gaps.testable_hypothesis: Adding entropy and agreement constraints will improve accuracy without increasing expected calibration error compared with raw confidence pseudo-labeling.
research_gaps.suggested_dataset: controlled SSL proxy with noisy labels and calibration metrics; remote follow-up: MedMNIST with ECE/Brier evaluation.
research_gaps.baseline_paper: [EV001] Pseudo-D: Informing Multi-View Uncertainty Estimation with Calibrated Neural Training Dynamics

research_gaps.text: The papers expose diagnostic uncertainty and unlabeled medical data as recurring problems, but many methods do not make pseudo-label rejection decisions auditable. The gap is a traceable reject-or-accept ledger for ambiguous unlabeled examples.
research_gaps.testable_hypothesis: A reject ledger based on entropy, margin, and disagreement will identify ambiguous samples that hurt baseline pseudo-label training.
research_gaps.suggested_dataset: sklearn_digits SSL proxy with saved selected/rejected pseudo-label tables.
research_gaps.baseline_paper: [EV001] Pseudo-D: Informing Multi-View Uncertainty Estimation with Calibrated Neural Training Dynamics

research_gaps.gap_title: Small-run reproducibility gap for SSL medical-image claims
research_gaps.text: Many relevant papers use medical datasets or segmentation pipelines that are expensive to reproduce. The gap is a MacBook-runnable proxy experiment that validates the mechanism before claiming medical-image contribution.
research_gaps.testable_hypothesis: A reproducible small proxy can falsify or support the pseudo-label selection mechanism before a remote medical benchmark is attempted.
research_gaps.suggested_dataset: sklearn_digits medical-image SSL proxy; remote_compute_needed for full medical benchmark reproduction.
research_gaps.baseline_paper: [EV001] Pseudo-D: Informing Multi-View Uncertainty Estimation with Calibrated Neural Training Dynamics

research_gaps.gap_title: Prior-art differentiation for uncertainty-aware SSL medical classification
research_gaps.text: The field contains many uncertainty-aware SSL methods, especially for segmentation. A contribution must differentiate classification-specific pseudo-label selection under label noise from existing consistency, co-training, and evidential-learning methods.
research_gaps.testable_hypothesis: A novelty tribunal comparing selection rule, uncertainty estimator, dataset setting, and failure mode will separate a narrow classification contribution from near-duplicate prior art.
research_gaps.suggested_dataset: literature-backed competitor table plus local proxy experiment.
research_gaps.baseline_paper: [EV009] Rethinking Semi-Supervised Medical Image Segmentation: A Variance-Reduction Perspective

method_matrix.title: Pseudo-D: Informing Multi-View Uncertainty Estimation with Calibrated Neural Training Dynamics
method_matrix.method: The method records model predictions across training epochs for each sample, aggregates these predictions into pseudo-labels representing model uncertainty, calibrates these pseudo-labels using validation data through temperature scaling or Dirichlet calibration, and trains models on these uncertainty-aware pseudo-labels to enhance robustness.
method_matrix.best_result: Pseudo-D achieves best study-level AURC of 0.885 and MAE of 0.180

method_matrix.title: Sam-Driven Weakly Supervised Nodule Segmentation with Uncertainty-Aware Cross Teaching
method_matrix.method: The framework first generates three bounding box prompts (tight box, ellipse-based, and circle-based) from clinical annotations to guide SAM in producing pseudo-labels. It then selects optimal pseudo-labels using intersection/union operations and employs uncertainty-aware cross-teaching between two models trained on these labels.
datasets_used: Thyroid Ultrasound Dataset (422 left + 422 right images)
Breast Ultrasound Dataset (604 left + 151 right images)
method_matrix.best_result: Ours achieved DSC of 73.5±22.2 and HD95 of 32.8±71.5 on Thyroid Ultrasound, and DSC of 75.5±19.8 and HD95 of 52.3±36.5 on Breast Ultrasound

method_matrix.title: Addressing Data Scarcity in 3D Trauma Detection through Self-Supervised and Semi-Supervised Learning with Vertex Relative Position Encoding
method_matrix.method: The approach uses patch-based Masked Image Modeling to pre-train a 3D U-Net encoder on unlabeled CT volumes, then employs this encoder for downstream tasks including 3D injury detection with VDETR and 3D RPE, and multi-label injury classification.
datasets_used: RSNA 2023 dataset (3,147 patients, 3,206 CT series for classification)
206 labeled CT volumes with 3D bounding box annotations for 3D injury detection (144 train, 30 val, 32 test)
2,000 unlabeled CT volumes for semi-supervised learning
method_matrix.best_result: Semi-supervised learning achieved 56.57% mAP@0.50 (114% improvement) for 3D injury detection

method_matrix.title: Mutual Evidential Deep Learning for Medical Image Segmentation
method_matrix.method: MEDL employs two segmentation networks with different architectures to generate complementary predictions for unlabeled data, fuses them via class-aware evidential fusion, calculates reliability using Shannon entropy, and applies Fisher information-based uncertainty weighting to guide targeted learning.
method_matrix.best_result: Ours: ACDC RV DSC 89.01%, JAC 80.80%, ASD 7.28mm, 95HD 1.70mm; LV DSC 93.34%, JAC 87.81%, ASD 4.25mm, 95HD 1.23mm; Avg DSC 90.40%, JAC 82.94%, ASD 3.38mm, 95HD 0.83mm
limitations: The paper acknowledges that model performance can be diminished by biases in model recognition caused by low-quality pseudo-labels in semi-supervised medical segmentation co-learning frameworks

method_matrix.title: SemSim: Revisiting Weak-to-Strong Consistency from a Semantic Similarity Perspective for Semi-supervised Medical Image Segmentation
method_matrix.method: Semi-supervised learning (SSL) for medical image segmentation is a challenging yet highly practical task, which reduces reliance on large-scale labeled dataset by leveraging unlabeled samples. Among S
datasets_used: ACDC (200 cardiac MRI images from 100 patients)
baselines_compared: MT, UA-MT, EM, DCT, CCT, CPS, ICT, DAN, URPC, SSNet, ICT-Med, FixMatch, SCPNet, BCP-Net, CTCT, S4CVnet, MedFCT, CnT-B, DiffRect, CPC-SAM
method_matrix.best_result: SemSim achieved 0.896 DSC and 2.3mm 95HD on ACDC with 10% labeled data

method_matrix.title: Rethinking Semi-Supervised Medical Image Segmentation: A Variance-Reduction Perspective
method_matrix.method: ARCO uses variance-reduced estimation with stratified group theory to improve contrastive learning for semi-supervised medical segmentation, incorporating specialized sampling techniques and multiple loss functions.
limitations: Focuses on medical image segmentation rather than classification
Framework designed specifically for contrastive learning scenarios
Does not explicitly address label noise challenges

method_matrix.title: EVIL: Evidential Inference Learning for Trustworthy Semi-Supervised Medical Image Segmentation
method_matrix.method: Recently, uncertainty-aware methods have attracted increasing attention in semi-supervised medical image segmentation. However, current methods usually suffer from the drawback that it is difficult to
datasets_used: Automated Cardiac Diagnosis Challenge (ACDC) dataset (200 annotated short-axis cardiac MR-cine images from 100 patients)
method_matrix.best_result: EVIL achieves DSC of 89.43% with 30% labeled data
limitations: The paper mentions that current methods struggle to balance computational cost, estimation accuracy, and theoretical support in a unified framework.

method_matrix.title: Semi-supervised learning for medical image classification using imbalanced training data
method_matrix.method: ABCL creates a blended target distribution that combines OSP and ASP predictions with weighting determined by class frequencies, pulling both original and augmented predictions toward this target distribution using Kullback-Leibler divergence loss.
method_matrix.best_result: UDA-WeightedCE-ABCL achieved UAR of 0.68, G-mean of 0.66, and average AUC of 0.96 on HAM10000 dataset

method_matrix.title: Exploring Feature Representation Learning for Semi-Supervised Medical Image Segmentation
method_matrix.method: The method employs a two-stage framework: first using boundary-aware contrastive loss on labeled data's boundary pixels to learn features, then applying prototype-aware contrastive loss on all pixels with pseudo labels. AUA estimates aleatoric uncertainty to adaptively weight consistency regularization between student and teacher models.
datasets_used: Colon tumor dataset (5 labeled, 95 unlabeled scans)
method_matrix.best_result: Ours achieves 79.81% Dice on Pancreas dataset (12 labeled, 48 unlabeled), surpassing state-of-the-art by 0.54%
method_matrix.compute_required: Training takes ~2x time vs single-stage methods; inference time similar to V-Net (4.70s vs 4.67s/sample)

method_matrix.title: Domain-Specific, Semi-Supervised Transfer Learning for Medical Imaging
method_matrix.method: MAKNet uses dense blocks with MAKConv layers, followed by standard conv, CBAM attention, and Mish activation. Semi-supervised training employs a teacher network (15 epochs) to generate pseudo-labels (>0.5 confidence) for unlabeled data, training a student network on concatenated labeled/unlabeled batches.
method_matrix.best_result: 0.9403 AUC on hand-labeled test set for LesaNet (TCIA) on DeepLesion dataset
limitations: Knowledge transfer across disparate domains is less effective

method_matrix.title: Learning Disentangled Stain and Structural Representations for Semi-Supervised Histopathology Segmentation
method_matrix.method: CSDS employs two specialized student networks (color and structure) trained on stain-augmented and structure-augmented inputs respectively, supervised by a shared teacher network updated via EMA, with uncertainty-weighted consistency losses.
method_matrix.best_result: CSDS achieves Dice of 85.06 ± 0.57 and Jaccard of 74.87 ± 0.82 on GlaS with 10% labeled data
limitations: Limited information available from the incomplete abstract

method_matrix.title: Uncertainty-Aware Extreme Point Tracing for Weakly Supervised Ultrasound Image Segmentation
method_matrix.method: The framework uses SAM2 with bounding box prompts derived from four extreme points to generate initial pseudo labels, which are progressively refined using an enhanced FGEPM algorithm with Monte Carlo dropout-based uncertainty estimation, while enforcing spatial consistency with USC loss and boundary alignment with box alignment loss.
datasets_used: UNS (Ultrasound Nerve Segmentation) dataset with 2,323 image-mask pairs
method_matrix.best_result: Our method achieved 67.70% IoU / 76.89% Dice on BUSI and 67.39% IoU / 79.12% Dice on UNS, surpassing fully supervised performance on BUSI
limitations: Limited to ultrasound images, may not generalize to other imaging modalities
Requires accurate identification of four extreme points for each structure
Uncertainty estimation relies on Monte Carlo dropout which may have limitations in capturing all sources of uncertainty

method_matrix.title: DRIVE: Dual-Robustness via Information Variability and Entropic Consistency in Source-Free Unsupervised Domain Adaptation
method_matrix.method: DRIVE employs a dual-model, two-stage approach with perturbation-induced robustness using Projected Gradient Descent (PGD) noise perturbations. Stage 1 aligns models on stable features using mutual information consistency loss, while Stage 2 dynamically adjusts perturbation levels based on Stage 1 losses.
limitations: Performance degradation on extremely low-resource target domains
Sensitivity to hyperparameters
Computational overhead of the proposed method
Limited evaluation on certain types of domain shifts

method_matrix.title: Anomaly Detection in Medical Imaging - A Mini Review
method_matrix.method: Qualitative literature review using Google Scholar and 4 search terms, filtering 120 papers to 45 relevant research items, then clustering by imaging modality and analyzing applications, methods, and motivations.
datasets_used: CT scans of skeleton or spines

method_matrix.title: Uncertainty-Guided Mutual Consistency Learning for Semi-Supervised Medical Image Segmentation
method_matrix.method: A dual-task network with segmentation and signed distance regression branches, using Monte Carlo Dropout for uncertainty estimation and enforcing both intra-task (student-teacher) and cross-task (segmentation-regression) consistency losses.
method_matrix.best_result: UG-MCL achieved 90.16% Dice on LA dataset with 16 labeled scans and 64 unlabeled scans
limitations: The method is primarily designed for segmentation tasks and may not directly apply to classification tasks.

method_matrix.title: Reliable fairness auditing with semi-supervised inference
method_matrix.method: Combines small labeled dataset with large unlabeled dataset by imputing missing outcomes via regression with nonlinear basis functions, then evaluates fairness criteria on the completed dataset.
method_matrix.best_result: Infairness achieves lowest bias and variance for ∆TPR estimation on MIMIC-III
limitations: Limited to fairness auditing applications rather than general semi-supervised learning
May not address all types of fairness criteria comprehensively
Assumes availability of unlabeled data which might not always be accessible

method_matrix.title: Less Is More: Surgical Phase Recognition From Timestamp Supervision
method_matrix.method: Uses Monte Carlo Dropout for uncertainty estimation, diffuses timestamp labels to adjacent low-uncertainty frames, and employs loop training to optimize spatial and temporal extractors separately using only labeled frames.
limitations: The method is specifically designed for surgical videos where phases are long events, which might limit its applicability to other medical image classification tasks.

method_matrix.title: Complementing Semi-Supervised Learning with Uncertainty Quantification
method_matrix.method: The approach proposes an unsupervised uncertainty-aware objective that quantifies aleatoric and epistemic uncertainty to align distributions between labeled and unlabeled domains, complementing existing SSL techniques without requiring computationally expensive graph formation or similarity computations.
limitations: Existing SSL methods do not incorporate uncertainty of pseudo labels or unlabeled samples
Issues with noisy labels and out-of-distribution samples due to strong augmentations

method_matrix.title: Uncertainty-Aware Semi-Supervised Few Shot Segmentation
method_matrix.method: Meta-train a neural network to jointly predict segmentation and uncertainty, then refine pseudo labels using uncertainty estimates during inference to generate reliable prototypes from unlabeled images.
datasets_used: PASCAL-5i (20 classes, 4 folds, 5 classes per fold)
COCO-20i (80 classes, 4 folds, 20 classes per fold)
method_matrix.best_result: Ours + Iq + R achieves 56.27% mean-IoU (1-shot) and 64.73% (5-shot) on PASCAL-5i with M=12 unlabeled images

method_matrix.title: Uncertainty-Aware Distillation for Semi-Supervised Few-Shot Class-Incremental Learning
method_matrix.method: The framework operates in incremental sessions: (1) CE module uses class-balanced self-training to incorporate unlabeled data while preventing class imbalance; (2) UaD module performs uncertainty-aware distillation between reference and target models using exemplars; (3) exemplars are updated by filtering samples from current datasets. The loss function combines cross-entropy and distillation losses (Equation 4).
method_matrix.best_result: UaD-CE achieves 54.50% on CIFAR100, 50.52% on miniImageNet, and 60.72% on CUB200 in the final session

metrics.idea_title: Uncertainty-calibrated pseudo-label acceptance under label noise
metrics.hypothesis: Entropy-filtered, class-balanced pseudo-label selection will improve held-out classification accuracy over naive confidence pseudo-labeling when the initial labeled set contains injected label noise.

metrics.idea_title: Class-balanced pseudo-labeling for scarce medical labels
metrics.hypothesis: Class-balanced low-entropy selection will reduce minority-class collapse and improve macro/weighted F1 against max-confidence selection.

metrics.idea_title: Calibration versus accuracy tradeoff in medical pseudo-label selection
metrics.hypothesis: Adding entropy and agreement constraints will improve accuracy without increasing expected calibration error compared with raw confidence pseudo-labeling.
